master_row.title: Measurement Back-Action
master_row.canonical_statement: Measuring a quantum system perturbs it; the operator expresses this disturbance, including curvature and TZP corrections.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as a series / summation relating ρ, k, M_k, I.
master_row.dictionary: The measurement back‑action operator (M) quantifies disturbance induced by measurement on a system: (M[] = _k M_k M_k^ - ), where (M_k) are measurement operators satisfying (_k M_k^ M_k = I); in the Trawinistic framework, (M_k) incorporate curvature‑dependent phases.
master_row.encyclopedia: Environment introduces decoherence and dissipation. Interpretive role: This entry makes the observational cost of measurement explicit. It also provides the bridge from noise spectra and decoherence into the operational quantum-information branch of the registry.
